editorsNote: Today's landscape is defined by a massive shift toward 'World Models' and agentic integration. While OpenAI and Anthropic battle for healthcare dominance with new patient-facing platforms, Google is doubling down on multimodal infrastructure and scientific reasoning with Gemini 3.1.

healthcareStories.headline: OpenAI and Anthropic Launch Rival Healthcare Platforms for Patients and Providers
healthcareStories.summary:
In a coordinated push into regulated medicine, OpenAI has launched 'ChatGPT Health' while Anthropic debuted 'Claude for Healthcare.' OpenAI's offering allows users to sync medical records via b.well and connect wellness apps like Apple Health and MyFitnessPal to receive grounded, longitudinal health insights. The company reports that 40 million people already use ChatGPT for health queries daily, and this new sandboxed environment adds privacy layers where data is not used for model training. The model was refined with input from over 260 physicians to prioritize safety and appropriate care escalation.

Anthropic’s 'Claude for Healthcare' targets both consumers and enterprises with a HIPAA-ready infrastructure. It features native 'Connectors' to the CMS Coverage Database, ICD-10 codes, and PubMed, allowing it to assist with complex administrative tasks like prior authorization and claims appeals. For consumers, Anthropic is integrating with HealthEx and Function Health to provide secure access to personal health records. Unlike general-purpose bots, these tools are designed to explain lab results in plain language and prepare patients for doctor visits.

Why it matters: This marks the transition of LLMs from general assistants to specialized clinical tools. For healthcare leaders, the focus shifts from 'if' AI will be used to 'how' to govern these new 'front doors' to the healthcare system. The competition between OpenAI and Anthropic will likely accelerate the adoption of AI-driven administrative automation and patient engagement, but it also raises significant questions about liability and the potential for misleading medical advice in complex, time-sensitive scenarios.
healthcareStories.source: OpenAI Blog / STAT News / Digital Health Wire

healthcareStories.headline: FDA Shifts 'Elsa' Assistant from Anthropic to Google Gemini Amid Legal Tensions
healthcareStories.summary:
The FDA has abruptly transitioned its generative AI assistant, 'Elsa,' from Anthropic’s Claude to Google’s Gemini model. Elsa is a critical tool used by FDA scientific reviewers to analyze regulatory submissions, identify data gaps, and set priorities. The shift follows a period of heightened tension between Anthropic and the U.S. government, including a lawsuit filed by Anthropic against the Department of War alleging retaliation after being displaced by OpenAI in defense contracts.

This move to Google Gemini (deployed within a FedRAMP-High environment) signals a broader realignment of federal AI partnerships. Life sciences sponsors are being advised to monitor how this change affects the 'logic' of regulatory reviews, as different foundation models may prioritize different data features during the automated screening of New Drug Applications (NDAs). The FDA's pivot highlights the volatility of the 'AI vendor stack' even at the highest levels of government.

Why it matters: For pharmaceutical executives, this change means the 'AI auditor' reviewing their data has changed its underlying reasoning engine. It underscores the importance of model-agnostic data preparation and the reality that political and legal disputes between AI labs can have immediate downstream effects on regulatory timelines.

healthcareStories.headline: Google DeepMind and Sanger Institute Launch AI Genomics Fellowship
healthcareStories.summary:
Google DeepMind has partnered with the Wellcome Sanger Institute to launch a new Academic Fellowship focused on applying AI to genomics. This first-of-its-kind program aims to support postdoctoral researchers in using advanced AI models to accelerate the understanding of biology, health, and disease. The fellowship will provide financial and mentoring resources, allowing researchers to leverage DeepMind's computational expertise alongside Sanger's large-scale genomic data generation capabilities.

The initiative is part of a broader strategy to move AI beyond abstract theory into 'transformative biological insight.' By focusing on underexplored areas of genomics, the partnership seeks to solve complex problems that have previously been limited by human analytical capacity. Applications for the program are set to open in late Spring 2026.

Why it matters: This represents a long-term investment in the 'AI for Science' pipeline. For R&D leaders, it signals that the next generation of precision medicine will be built by researchers who are equally fluent in CRISPR and Transformer architectures. It also reinforces Google's position as the primary infrastructure provider for the future of digital biology.

techStories.headline: Yann LeCun Co-Founds AMI Labs, Raises $1B for 'World Model' AI
techStories.summary:
Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI), a new startup co-founded by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has raised $1.03 billion in a record-breaking seed round. The company aims to move beyond the 'token prediction' limits of current LLMs to develop 'World Models'—AI that genuinely understands and interacts with three-dimensional physical reality. The round, led by Bezos Expeditions and Greycroft, values the Paris-based startup at $3.5 billion.

LeCun argues that current generative architectures merely mimic intelligence without understanding the physical laws of the world. AMI's goal is to build systems that can learn from video and sensory data to predict physical outcomes, a capability essential for advanced robotics and autonomous systems. This follows a similar $1 billion raise by Fei-Fei Li's 'World Labs,' signaling a massive investor pivot toward 'Spatial Intelligence.'

Why it matters: This is a direct challenge to the 'scaling laws' of text-based AI. If AMI succeeds, it could unlock the next level of automation in manufacturing, surgery, and logistics—areas where current LLMs struggle due to a lack of physical grounding. It marks the beginning of the 'Post-LLM' era in AI research.

techStories.headline: OpenAI Acquires Promptfoo to Standardize AI Agent Governance
techStories.summary:
OpenAI has acquired Promptfoo, a leading open-source platform for testing and evaluating LLM outputs. Promptfoo will be integrated into OpenAI’s 'Frontier' platform, a new orchestration layer designed for managing enterprise AI agents. The acquisition is a strategic move to provide 'one-stop governance,' allowing CIOs to generate automated security and compliance reports for their AI workflows.

Promptfoo is widely used for 'red-teaming'—the process of stress-testing models against prompt injections and hallucinations. By folding this into their core offering, OpenAI aims to make integrated security a baseline requirement for enterprise procurement. OpenAI representatives noted that risk scores will now be surfaced directly beside agent logs, providing real-time monitoring of agentic behavior.

Why it matters: As enterprises move from simple chatbots to autonomous agents that touch critical workflows, 'evals' (evaluations) have become the most important part of the stack. This acquisition signals that OpenAI is moving from being a model provider to a full-stack enterprise software company, competing directly with traditional governance and security vendors.

techStories.headline: Google Completes $23B Acquisition of Wiz to Secure Multicloud AI
techStories.summary:
Google has finalized its acquisition of Wiz, the cloud security giant, for a reported $23 billion. Wiz will join Google Cloud but maintain its independent brand. The deal is the largest in Google's history and is designed to create a 'unified security platform' that protects AI models and data across multicloud environments (AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud).

The integration will focus on detecting emerging AI-generated threats and protecting the 'AI supply chain'—from training data to runtime environments. Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian emphasized that as organizations adopt agentic AI, the attack surface expands, requiring a security layer that can 'hunt for threats' using AI itself. Wiz’s ability to provide a consistent security posture across different clouds is seen as a major competitive advantage for Google.

Why it matters: This is a defensive and offensive masterstroke. It secures Google's own AI infrastructure while giving them a foothold in the security budgets of companies primarily using AWS or Azure. For executives, it simplifies the 'security of AI' by providing a single pane of glass for multicloud governance.

socialHighlights.content: My prediction: 'World Models' will be the next buzzword. In six months, every company will call itself a world model to raise funding. But genuine understanding of 3D reality is much harder than predicting the next token. AMI is here to build the real thing.

Open-sourced 'AutoResearch' today. It's a simple loop for running AI-driven research on a single GPU. Agents modify code, run training experiments, and evaluate results. It's not AGI, but it's a very useful 'grad student' in a box for iterative ML work.

I'm thrilled to announce 'Context Hub' in the latest Batch. Agentic coding systems fail because they don't know about APIs released after their training cutoff. Context Hub gives them the documentation they need to write correct code in real-time.

googlePocItems.title: Building a Multimodal Medical Lab Interpreter
googlePocItems.description: Create a tool that uses Gemini 1.5 Pro to interpret photos of lab results and explain them in patient-friendly language while flagging values for physician review.

guide.instruction: Enable the Vertex AI API in your Google Cloud Console and upload a sample (de-identified) lab result image to a GCS bucket.
Use the Vertex AI SDK to initialize Gemini 1.5 Pro with a system instruction focused on medical clarity and safety disclaimers.
guide.codeSnippet: model = GenerativeModel('gemini-1.5-pro-002', system_instruction='You are a medical lab assistant. Explain results clearly but always include a disclaimer that you are not a doctor.')

guide.instruction: Prompt the model to extract specific biomarkers and provide a 'Patient Summary' and a 'Physician Flag' list in JSON format.
guide.codeSnippet: response = model.generate_content([image_part, 'Extract all values. Provide a JSON with keys: biomarker, value, reference_range, patient_explanation, flag_for_doctor (boolean).'])

googlePocItems.title: Agentic Clinical Trial Matcher
googlePocItems.description: Deploy a Vertex AI Agent that searches ClinicalTrials.gov via API to match patient profiles to active studies.

guide.instruction: In Agent Builder, create a new 'Search' agent and define a 'Tool' that connects to the ClinicalTrials.gov REST API.
Set the agent's goal to: 'Identify the top 3 matching trials for this patient profile based on inclusion/exclusion criteria.'
Input a synthetic patient profile (e.g., '65yo male, Stage III NSCLC, failed first-line chemo') and observe the agent's multi-step search and filtering process.

deepLearningSpotlight.title: Context Hub: Solving the 'Knowledge Cutoff' for Coding Agents
deepLearningSpotlight.summary:
In the latest edition of 'The Batch,' Andrew Ng introduces 'Context Hub,' a tool designed to address a primary failure point in agentic coding: the lack of up-to-date API documentation. Because LLMs are frozen in time at the end of their training, they often hallucinate calls for libraries or tools that have changed. Context Hub acts as a real-time retrieval layer that feeds the latest documentation directly into the agent's context window.

Andrew Ng emphasizes that the future of AI isn't just about bigger models, but about 'agentic workflows' that can look up information as needed. He argues that providing 'just-in-time' context is more efficient and accurate than trying to fine-tune models on every new software update. This approach allows developers to build coding assistants that remain useful even as the underlying tech stack evolves daily.
deepLearningSpotlight.author: Andrew Ng

deepLearningSpotlight.title: Aletheia: Google's Agent for Original Mathematical Research
deepLearningSpotlight.summary:
DeepLearning.AI highlights 'Aletheia,' a new agentic system from Google that uses Gemini 3 Deep Think to solve 'stubborn' math problems. While LLMs have previously achieved gold-medal performance in math competitions, Aletheia goes further by engaging in iterative research—generating hypotheses, testing them against formal verifiers, and revising its logic. 

The technical breakthrough lies in the 'Deep Think' reasoning mode, which allows the model to spend significantly more compute time on a single problem, exploring multiple 'chains of thought' before arriving at a solution. The Batch team notes that this 'human-on-the-loop' approach, where the AI does the heavy lifting of exploration and the human mathematician provides high-level guidance, is becoming the standard for scientific AI.
deepLearningSpotlight.author: The Batch Team

generalLearningItems.title: Designing AI Agents to Resist Prompt Injection
generalLearningItems.summary: A new technical guide from OpenAI's security team on building robust system prompts and using 'instruction hierarchy' to prevent users from hijacking agent behavior.

generalLearningItems.title: Gemini Embedding 2: Multimodal Retrieval Guide
generalLearningItems.summary: Documentation and code samples for Google's first natively multimodal embedding model, which allows for searching across text, images, and video in a single vector space.
